what_it_does: Declares one RELATION_TYPE dict registering the 'attachment' board edge-kind: a directed item->artifact link (to an image://, blob:// address) with inverse 'attached_to'.
observations: Module docstring describes the edge as: this item carries an attached artifact (image/file) at image://<channel>/<path>.

Docstring narrates the use case: a comment carries images the operator attached on the phone; the review surface renders them inline; an agent receiving the comment gets the attachment address in the envelope.
desc: the source item (e.g. a comment) has an attached artifact at the target address (image://, blob://).
standouts: Declares inverse='attached_to', pairing this edge with the separately-defined attached_to row.
Docstring ties the edge to a concrete end-to-end flow: phone attach -> inline render -> agent envelope.
conventions: Single module-level RELATION_TYPE constant, id matching filename.
Adds an inverse key (unlike the minimal attached_to.py row), using the optional schema fields.
concerns: Declares inverse='attached_to', but attached_to.py is an item->channel edge with its own meaning (not the inverse of an item->artifact edge); the inverse pairing may be semantically overloaded — unclear from this file alone whether that is intended.
notes: The 'attachment' edge: a board item (e.g. a comment) points to an attached image/blob artifact, with inverse attached_to.
questions: Is inverse='attached_to' intentionally reusing the channel-attachment edge id, or is it meant to invert artifact attachment specifically?
What address schemes beyond image:// and blob:// are valid targets?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — registers the attachment edge-kind described in its docstring.
apparent_themes: Carrying operator-attached media through to agents and review surfaces
intention_signals: Tries to make an attached image/file a first-class typed link from a board item so the same attachment flows from phone capture to inline rendering to the agent's envelope.
novelty: The address-scheme targets (image://, blob://) and the explicit phone->render->agent-envelope flow encoded in a registry row's docstring.
contribution: Contributes the 'attachment' edge-kind row enabling board items (comments) to carry typed references to attached image/blob artifacts.
summary_for_embedding: A board_edges row declaring the directed 'attachment' edge-kind linking a board item (e.g. a comment) to an attached image/blob artifact, with inverse attached_to.
intention_for_embedding: To let board items carry operator-attached images/files as typed edges that render inline and reach agents via the envelope.
